text: The Hebrew text here is difficult, and may perhaps be corrupt; for ‘oppression withered up from the roots’ it has ‘bring low the song of the oppressor’.
For ‘ground in the chaff-cutter’ the Hebrew text has ‘trodden down in the dung-hill’ (or perhaps, the cess-pool).

It is not easy, either in the Latin or in the Hebrew text, to be certain what the subject of the verbs is, or what the precise picture is meant to be. In the Latin it is, apparently, that of a man who puts out his hands to save himself from falling, but unsuccessfully.
